    • EDA: Graph-based Features from Spatial Cell Data

Add graph features to a MultiAssayExperiment

Source: R/importGraphFeatures.R
addGraphToMAE.Rd

Integrates a SummarizedExperiment of patient-level graph features (produced by importGraphFeatures) into an existing MultiAssayExperiment, aligning by patient ID.

Usage

addGraphToMAE(mae, graph_se, experiment_name = "graph_features")

Arguments

mae

A MultiAssayExperiment.

graph_se

A SummarizedExperiment produced by importGraphFeatures, with patient IDs as column names.

experiment_name

Character(1). Name of the new experiment slot in the returned MultiAssayExperiment. Default "graph_features".

Value

A MultiAssayExperiment with the graph features added as an additional experiment.

Details

Only patients present in colData(mae) are retained in graph_se. The sampleMap is constructed automatically, assuming that column names of graph_se correspond directly to the primary sample identifiers in mae.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
library(MultiAssayExperiment)
# mae <- curatedTCGAData::curatedTCGAData("PRAD", "RNASeq2GeneNorm*", ...)
# graph_se <- importGraphFeatures("path/to/features.csv")
mae2 <- addGraphToMAE(mae, graph_se)
experiments(mae2)
} # }
